sections.paragraph:
ἐπιπλήσσω (s. πλήσσω); 1 aor. ἐπέπληξα; prim. sense ‘strike at’ (since Hom.), also rebuke, reprove (Hom. et al.; Pla., Polyb.; BGU 1138, 22 [19 b.c.]) τινί someone (Il. 12, 211; Epict., Ench. 33, 16; Appian, Liby. 65 §291, Bell. Civ. 5, 13 §51; Philo, Leg. All. 2, 46; Jos., Ant. 1, 246; 19, 346) 1 Ti 5:1. Also τινά (‘the tragedy’ in Simplicius in Epict. p. 95, 43 ἐπιπλήττουσί με; Lucian, Herm. 20; UPZ 15, 32 [156 b.c.]) πάντας δὲ οὓς ἐθεράπευσεν ἐπέπληξεν all whom he healed he rebuked Mt 12:15f v.l. (ASyn. 113, mg.). Abs. (X., Oec. 13, 12) Lk 23:43 D (where ἐπλήσοντι is to be corrected to ἐπιπλήσσοντι).—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

prim. prim. = primarily

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Simplicius Simplicius , VI a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

ASyn. ASyn. = KAland, Synopsis Quattuor Evangeliorum13—Lists 1, 6

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 377
